title: quercus costaricensis
intro: Quercus costaricensis, also known as the Costa Rican white oak, is a species of deciduous tree that belongs to the beech family (Fagaceae). The medicinal properties of Quercus costaricensis lie in its bark, which contains compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of this plant include the potential to lower cholesterol levels, reduce blood pressure, and protect against cardiovascular disease, as well as exhibit antimicrobial and antifungal properties. Herbal preparations of Quercus costaricensis can be found in the form of tea, capsules, or extracts, which are often used to support cardiovascular health and immune function.
